Now I have all the information needed to compare the documented and implemented endpoints.

## API Endpoint Comparison

| Endpoint | Method | Documentation Status | Implementation Status |
|----------|--------|---------------------|----------------------|
| `/api/users` | GET | ✅ Documented | ✅ Implemented |
| `/api/users` | POST | ✅ Documented | ✅ Implemented |
| `/api/health` | GET | ✅ Documented | ✅ Implemented |

### Summary

**All documented endpoints are implemented:**

1. **`GET /api/users`** — Listed in README, implemented in `UsersController.scala` (handle method returns JSON array of users with status 200)

2. **`POST /api/users`** — Listed in README, implemented in `UsersController.scala` (handle method returns `{"status":"created"}` with status 201; has a TODO for parsing/validation)

3. **`GET /api/health`** — Listed in README, implemented directly in `Main.scala` (returns `"OK"` with status 200)

**No discrepancies found.** The documentation accurately reflects what is implemented in the source code. All three endpoints exist in both the README and the code.
